observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to review the depreciation expenses applied to the Road Networks account and prepare necessary adjusting entries to correct the identified misstatements.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reclassify the completed projects to its appropriate PPE account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile their respective reports and records for LDRRMF.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer transfer the current year unexpended QRF and DRRMF- MOOE to the Special Trust Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the mentioned semi-expendable properties to the appropriate semi-expendable account in the books and Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require Municipal Accountant to:

a. Prepare the necessary journal entries to record the valid reconciling items in the books;

b. Cause the reconciliation of the total amounts of CIB-LCCA between subsidiary ledgers and general ledger under the General Fund; and,

c. Prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements on all bank accounts maintained by the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:

a. The Municipal Accountant to ensure that all proceeding disbursements thru e-Payments are based on a duly approved DVs/Payrolls;

b. The Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit Daily Report of e-Payments from the Agency Account; and

c. The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the understatement of the CIB-LCCA and Due to Officers and Employees accounts, both by P69,947.07.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a) Require the officers and employees to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period;

b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to strengthen internal control measures over cash advances to ensure timely submission of liquidation documents and full year’s accounting of accountabilities of accountable officers; and

c) Direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting cash advances to employees if the previous cash advance is not yet settled; and

d) Require the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to the accountable officers to settle their unliquidated cash advances and refund all unexpended balances, if any.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for all taxes withheld, and remit the taxes withheld regularly and within the prescribed period.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee to submit copies of the contracts to the Audit Team within five (5) days from execution and issuance thereof.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to facilitate the implementation of the remaining development programs and projects.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer and LDRRMO to ensure that the LDRRMF is utilized solely for disaster risk reduction and management activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other members of the GAD Focal Point System ensure the implementation of GAD-related PPAs through proper gender mainstreaming or attribution of GAD fund to the programs, projects and activities of the LGU, to fully address the gender sensitive issues in the locality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. Municipal Accountant to impose collection of liquidated damages from the suppliers who incurred delays in deliveries; and

b. BAC Chairman to ensure that the terms and conditions indicated in the contracts are strictly complied with.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements for infrastructure projects are supported with complete documentation.

We also recommended that the BAC Chairperson submit the bidding documents, contracts and all documents forming part thereof, in accordance Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the immediate operation of the newly constructed Super Health Center.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor coordinate with NGCP for the relocation of the site of the electric post if the same poses dangers to the users of the Health Center.
We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the identified persons responsible or liable in the Notices of Suspension and Notices of Disallowances to settle the NS and ND.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Direct the General Services Officer to prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI), together with the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS), to the Accounting Office for recognition of expense at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and Disbursing Officer to do the following:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Create an Oversight Committee to: (1) review the current fees and charges imposed by the LGU; (2) compute the appropriation rates for each services rendered by the LGU, and (3) submit to the Municipal Mayor the proposed revisions of the rates and charges;

b. Require the appointed Chairperson of the Oversight Committee, to recommend to the SB the immediate approval of the proposed revisions of the LGU’s Municipal Revenue Code; and

c. Request from the Municipal Vice-mayor, as the Presiding Officer of the SB to facilitate the immediate issuance of a local revenue ordinance for the adoption of the revised rates on fees and charges of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor constitute an Internal Audit Service Unit in the organizational structure of the Municipality of Lavezares and hire qualified/competent personnel therein to perform the functions required by the said office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

The Municipal Treasurer to secure from the Authorized Government Depository Banks the needed copies of bank statements and take appropriate or alternative actions to determine the balances of bank accounts at the end of each month for the purpose of the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statement. If the bank statements are not available, the management should request for the electronic copies of its transactions;

The Municipal Accountant to prepare journal entries to record the valid reconciling items in the books in order to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account and other related accounts affected in the financial statements; and

The Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement on the four (4) bank accounts of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

The Inventory Committee to prepare a systematic and well-planned physical inventory taking, which could be done in a year-round basis, until all the Property, Plant and Equipment of the municipality have been accounted for, and to prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and ensure that all the necessary data are provided thereon;

The General Service Officer to ensure that all PPEs owned by the municipality are duly recorded in the Property Cards (PCs) maintained by the Property Unit and are provided with property stickers with the vital information of the PPE; and

The Inventory Committee, in coordination with the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant, to conduct one-time cleansing of PPE Accounts to come up with a reliable PPE balance that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to correct the balances of the affected accounts, and submit to the Audit Team the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher, for verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant the following courses of action, to wit:

Revert the erroneous debit to Due to NGAs the progress and final payments, as well as the payment of 10% Retention, to CIP-Buildings and Other Structures account;

Recognize the appropriate PPE account of the completed Super Health Center; and

Transfer to the General Fund the PPE, to close the Due to NGAs and Due to Other Funds account for the counterpart fund.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to henceforth, follow the prescribed accounting entries for payment of mobilization fee, progress and final payments, and release of 10% Retention.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments in the Trust Fund books to correct the erroneous recording of the receipt of CY 2022 unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements for infrastructure projects are supported with complete documentation.

We also recommended that the BAC Chairperson submit bidding documents, contracts and all documents forming part thereof, in accordance Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the immediate operation of the newly constructed Super Health Center.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor coordinate with NGCP for the relocation of the site of the electric post if the same poses dangers to the users of the Health Center.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to require the contractor of the said project to rectify the deficiency observed, otherwise, a refund of the amount corresponding to the unperformed work should be demanded.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile their respective reports and records for LDRRMF.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer transfer the current year unexpended QRF and DRRMF- MOOE to the Special Trust Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to submit distribution list for the relief goods distributed and, henceforth, ensure that issuances of relief goods to beneficiaries are supported with duly verified Distribution List or its equivalent document.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that control procedures and complete documentation are established for claims against government funds.
We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the identified persons responsible or liable in the Notices of Suspension and Notices of Disallowances to settle the audit suspensions/disallowances.
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to correct the said transaction.

a. Constitute an Inventory Committee that will conduct the physical count of
the local road networks of the municipality and prepare and submit the
RPCLRN not later than January 31 of each year;
b. Require the GSO to prepare at the end of each accounting period a Report
on Local Road Network;
c. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and
the GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects; and
d. Require the Municipal Accountant to provide the required disclosure in the
Notes to FS as required by COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to:
1. Coordinate with the Municipal Engineer on the status of the projects of the
municipality by identifying all completed projects and construction in
progress, analyze the Construction in Progress account for reclassification to
the proper PPE account; and
2. Observe strict compliance with the proper recording of transactions
involving acquisition of infrastructure projects as well as the maintenance of
its subsidiary records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following officials:
a) The Municipal Treasurer to turn-over the responsibility of filing and
remittance of taxes to the Accounting Office; and
b) The Municipal Accountant to submit the documentary requirements to
support the accounting entries on the remittance of taxes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. Reconcile the total of the balances in the subsidiary ledgers with the balance
in the General Ledger by exerting efforts to identify the transactions which
caused the difference of P9,677,874.51;
b. Exert effort to ascertain the errors which brought the negative balance of
Due to NGAs, and cause the immediate correction of the same;
c. Ascertain the programs, projects and activities of the fund transfers through
the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) and/or any communication received
from source agency; and
d. Prepare and submit the Fund Utilization Report for each fund transfer, to
identify unused or excesses of funds in the hands of the municipality and
cause the immediate return of these funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct FJM Pharma to post the
required Warranty Security equivalent to 10% of the contract price, to be
released only after the medicines are consumed.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the posting of
applicable warranty security in all and succeeding procurements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairman and
BAC Secretary to submit copies of approved contracts within five (5) days after
their execution as provided for in COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2009-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. The Municipal Accountant to adopt perpetual inventory methods in
accounting for the acquisition of inventories held for distribution; and
b. The GSO-designate and other concerned offices to summarize the receipt,
distribution and balance of relief goods in the monthly Report of Supplies and
Materials Issued (RSMI) and submit to the accounting unit together with the
copies of the Distribution Sheets of relief goods for proper recording of the
issuances of inventory items for distribution.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. The Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization
of DRRMF and to provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to FS following
the format prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
b. The LDRRM Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of
DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MENRO to the following
courses of action:
a) Follow-up the approval of the Solid Waste Management Plan by the National
Solid Waste Management Commission to achieve its vision of understanding
the environmental benefits of waste reduction and proper waste disposal;
b) Provide all collectors and other personnel directly dealing with collection of
solid waste with personal protective equipment to protect them from the
hazards of handling wastes; and
c) Enforce the proper segregation of solid waste at source thru the conduct of
information and dissemination campaign on proper solid waste management,
so that the constituents will be fully aware of the benefits derived from the
program.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Officer
– Designate to the following courses of action:
a) Prepare and submit PAR for the equipment issued to end-users to establish
their accountability; and
b) Keep copies of the PAR on file for control and monitoring purposes.

We recommended, however, that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Engineer to require the contractor of the said project to rectify the deficiency
observed, otherwise, a refund of the amount corresponding to the unperformed
work should be demanded.

1.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare journal entries to correct the accounting errors in the
financial statements, and copies of the JEVs be furnished to the Audit Team
for purposes of verification. We further recommended that the Municipal
Accountant exercise prudence in the recording of transactions to minimize
erroneous entries.

2.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to adopt perpetual inventory method in accounting for
purchases of inventories.

3.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant the following:
a) Ensure that the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) are prepared for all
transactions of the municipality, as this shall serve as basis for recording
the transactions in the General Journal and Special Journals; and
b) Ascertain compliance in accomplishing the Subsidiary Ledgers as to the
details and breakdown of transactions posted therein, pursuant to the Manual on the
New Government Accounting System.

4.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following:
a) The Municipal Accountant to maintain supplies ledger cards to monitor
the issuances of the inventories;
b) Designate a General Services Officer for the maintenance of stock cards,
and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI), together with
the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS), to the Accounting Office for recognition
of expense of issued/distributed inventories; and
c) Create an inventory committee for the conduct of physical count of
inventories every semester and submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories
(RPCI) within the prescribed period.

5.7 We reiterated our audit recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Constitute the Inventory Committee that will conduct the physical
inventory of all PPEs following the prescribed composition of the
committee provided in Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated
October 20, 1992, in order to prepare RPCPPE following the prescribed
forms in the Manual for NGAS; and
b. Direct Municipal Treasurer, in the absence of Supply/Property Officer to
maintain Property Cards.

6.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal
Accountant and HRMO to ensure that paid payrolls be duly supported with
the required supporting documents as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-
001 dated June 14, 2012.

7.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards
Committee Chairperson, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to
submit the lacking documents in the contracts and final payments of the
three (3) infrastructure projects funded under Local Government Support
Fund.

8.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards
Committee Chairperson, in coordination with BAC Secretariats and Municipal
Accountant, to submit the lacking documentary requirements, in
compliance to GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 and GPPB
Resolution No. 18-2020 dated September 16, 2020, respectively.

9.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to do the
following:
a) Submit the original or certified true copy of certification from Punong
Barangay; and
b) Provide in a document the determination of vulnerable sector to which
the beneficiaries belong, to determine the documentary requirements
needed in the pay-out of like assistance, in the future, if any.

10.11 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRMO to
ensure that the LDRRMF is utilized solely for disaster risk reduction and
management activities.

10.12 We also recommended that the MSWDO make sure that issuance of relief
goods to beneficiaries, be supported with Requisition and Issue Slip,
Inventory Custodian Slip, duly validated Distribution List, or equivalent
document.

11.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to submit Gas Issue Slips, Drivers Trip Tickets, Monthly
Report of Official Travels, and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for CY
2020 fuel requisitions and consumptions.

12.6 We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to take the following courses of
actions:
a. direct the BAC Chairperson to strictly adhere to the procurement procedures
set by RA 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR and to pertinent GPPB issuances;
b. require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the BAC
Chairperson, to submit the lacking supporting documents.

13.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the leeway and
flexibility afforded on the utilization of the 20% DF be optimally utilized for
the benefit and welfare of the constituent.

14.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the District Supervisor
to fully utilize the Special Education Fund Budget in conformity to DepEd-
DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017, DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint
Circular No. 1, series of 2020, and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 2,
series of 2020 in order to provide all the schools within the district the
necessary equipage in response to the BE-LCP program of the DepEd.

We recommended that: a) Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the collection to recover government funds invested in the program and instruct the Municipal Accountant to institute action for the collection of the accounts or other appropriate remedies to clear the amount in the books. b) Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to determine the reasons on why each fund transfer account balance were not liquidated and undertake immediate appropriate action. If warranted, request/apply for the write-off of the accounts following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016. c) Municipal Mayor: a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to institute action for the collection of the accounts or other appropriate remedies to clear the amount in the books; b) Analyze each fund transfer account balance and determine the reason why these were not liquidated and undertake immediate appropriate action thereon; and c) If warranted, request/apply for the write-off of the accounts following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: AAR Not Implemented 2018 2017 The LGU did not take any action to implement this audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) Give priority for the creation of an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of PPE; b) Direct the head of the Municipal Treasurer Office and the Municipal Accounting Office to hasten the updating of their records to facilitate the reconciliation of the accounting and property records; and c) Oblige the Municipal Accountant to review the details of properties which are non-functional or unserviceable and prepare the necessary adjustments, if warranted; d) Require the committee to prepare a methodical and well-planned physical inventory taking, which could be done in a year-round basis, until all the Property, Plant and Equipment of the municipality have been accounted for and listed in the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). Also, all AREs must be renewed regularly pursuant to existing rules and regulations; e) Thereafter, the RPCPPE must be reconciled with the property record of each class of property at the General Services Office and subsidiary ledgers maintained at the Accounting Office. Any discrepancy should be investigated and appropriate action should be taken thereof; f) Make representation with the previous accounting personnel to substantiate and reconcile the forwarded balances prior to CY 2012.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) Property Custodian to update, prepare and submit all documents and reports such as duly approved RIS to evidence the issuance of the materials from stock and Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI); and b) Municipal Accountant to refrain from recording the consumed inventory from the books and reconcile the accounting and property and supply records; c) Adjust the non-existing and consumed unaccounted materials inventory in the books. b) Municipal Accountant to: • Comply with the provisions of Sections 43 of the Manual of the NGAS, Volume I on the proper recording of purchases and issuances of supplies and materials; and • Maintain the Subsidiary Ledger Card (SLC) for inventories. c) Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of inventory items under the perpetual inventory method in its related inventory account and also the Municipal Social Welfare Officer and Treasurer to furnish the copy of delivery receipt/acceptance and inspection report of all received inventory items.
observations.managementAction: AAR Not Implemented 2018 2017 The LGU did not take any action and the required documents were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the expired portion from the prepayments and prepare periodically liquidation report of all prepayments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-Agency Accounts, promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and effect the necessary adjustments, if appropriate, to bring the affected accounts into an agreement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU observe the provisions of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC 2017-001 and ensure the Local School Board to come up with a strategic prioritization plan in the allocation of the SEF to public schools taking into consideration the most important needs of the students and not just for repairs of school facilities for optimum attainment of the intent of the fund in accordance with DepEd-DDBM-DLG JC 2017-001.
observations.managementAction: AAR Not Implemented 2018 The SEF budget was likewise not fully utilized. (See related finding in Part II of this Report)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit the taxes withheld intact to lessen the liability and accountability of the LGU, and prevent the possibility of using the funds for other purposes.

observations.recommendation: Since fiscal responsibility rests upon the Head of the Agency, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to render her accounts and submit the required financial transactions together with supporting documents to the Accounting Division, and the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts and reports to COA on or before the 10th day of the succeeding month.
observations.managementAction: AAR Not Implemented 2017 2016 2014 The LGU acknowledged delays and indicated actions to improve submission but permanent staff to assist were lacking.
observations.reason: Lacking permanent staff to assist in the preparation of FS and timely reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Reconciliation between actual amounts on a comparable basis and include data for the Special Education Fund in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts. Henceforth, strictly comply the provisions of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in the preparation of the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management submit the lacking supporting documents; rectify the deficiencies noted; require the contractors/suppliers to issue official receipt in gross amount; and implement projects within the specified contract time. Henceforth, refrain from procuring thru reimbursements, payment without complete documentations, and strictly comply the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR. Also submit justification for the early period of action on procurement activities and why COA was not informed of the deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the required Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreement of the different projects as well as the Project Briefs and Local Poverty Reduction Action Plan (LPRAP) for the livelihood projects. Further, projects be fully implemented upon receipt of the funds for the benefit of the constituents. Also rectify deficiencies noted.
observations.managementAction: AAR Not Implemented 2016 The required Project Briefs and LPRAP were not submitted to the audit team. Some projects not implemented or not fully implemented though funds received.
observations.reason: Project Briefs and LPRAP not submitted; some projects not implemented though funds received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU assess its existing organizational structure so that the ideal number of plantilla personnel will be established, thereby, avoiding the hiring of excessive number of job order personnel. We further recommended that the hiring of job order personnel be resorted to based on necessity in order to avoid waste of much needed funds, as provided in Section 76 of R.A. 7160. Likewise, the LGU should strictly adhere to the policies and guidelines in the hiring of personnel under contract of services and job orders as provided in CSC Memorandum No. 17, s. 2002.
observations.managementAction: AAR Not Implemented 2015 The LGU acknowledged the issue and committed to review staffing requirements but had not yet implemented formal organizational adjustments or hiring policy reforms.
observations.reason: No formal organizational structure/staffing plan or written hiring policy implemented yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management refrain from disbursement of government fund without complete documentations in compliance with Section 2 (6) of PD 1445, COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 12, 2012, and Section 10 of R.A. 9184; and ensure that projects are implemented per applicable rules and that complete supporting documents are provided.
observations.managementAction: AAR Not Implemented 2015 The management has not submitted the required documents. Some projects lacked complete supporting documents.
observations.reason: Required supporting documents not submitted for trust fund disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary reconciliation and adjusting entries to rectify the abnormal/negative balance in the financial statements in compliance with COA Cir. No. 2004-008 dated September 20, 2004 and Section 111 of PD 1445. Furthermore, the LGU should refrain from using government funds to pay personal loans of employees without salary deduction. Require concerned employees to immediately settle personal loans paid by the LGU even without deduction from their salary.
observations.managementAction: AAR Not Implemented 2014 The Municipal Accountant will conduct reconciliation and prepare necessary adjustments. As of 12/31/2019 the liability accounts with negative balances totaled ₱405,579.55.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Assessor to update the real property tax assessment roll and prepare/send the Real Property Tax Order of Payment (RPTOP) to individual taxpayers. Also require the Municipal Treasurer to exert earnest efforts to intensify collection in order to generate more income to finance the LGU basic services. Moreover, require the Local Finance Committee to determine the reasonableness of the income projected as collectible for every ensuing fiscal year.

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We recommended for the Municipal Accountant to submit the Statement of
Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) and prepare the required
reconciliation which is to be disclosed either on the face of the SCBAA or in the
Notes to Financial Statements, in compliance with the provisions of IPSAS 1 and
IPSAS 24.

2.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
the following courses of actions:
1. to maintain subsidiary ledgers for each depositary account under the
Cash in Bank - LCCA control account;
2. to prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation statement (BRS)
together with paid checks, original copies of debit/credit memos within
10 days after end of each month for each account; and
3. to submit the DVs and supporting documents of the newly opened bank
accounts.

3.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the General Services Officer to prepare at the end of each
accounting period, a Report on Local Road Network and maintain a
Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
b) Require the Municipal Engineer and the Inventory Committee to submit
the required reports so that the Municipal Accountant can record all the
road network owned by the municipality to ensure that all roads are
properly valued and recorded, and to assure the fair presentation of the
Road Networks in the financial statements; and
c) Require the Municipal Accountant to compute the accumulated
depreciation for all the road networks owned by the Municipality and
provide the disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements for the
succeeding year as required by COA Circular COA Circular No. 2015-
008.

4.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to prepare journal entry to correct the understatements in Due to BIR and Cash
in Bank -LCCA accounts. Also, may we be furnished of the JEVs to record the
adjusting entries for purposes of verification.

5.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to provide the depreciation for Property, Plant and Equipment acquired in the
current and prior years in accordance to IPSAS 17 and Philippine Application
Guidance to IPSAS 17. The Municipal Accountant should also verify the
difference of the depreciation expense recorded in the lapsing schedule and in
the financial statements for the CY 2019.

We recommended that: a) The Municipal Mayor to direct the Petty Cash custodian to affect immediate liquidation of the petty cash fund; and b) The Municipal Mayor to: a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to update the RPTARs/Taxpayers’ Index by the Card and subsequently, prepare and furnish the Accounting Office the list required under Section 20 Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and b) Municipal Accountant to establish RPT and SET receivables sourcing from the list duly certified by the Treasury Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned officials and employees immediately submit the lacking disbursement vouchers, payrolls and liquidation reports with supporting documents, the cancelled check, a report on the actual status of checks noted as gaps in the series of checks issued and the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms. observations.recommendation: We recommended refund of PEI from the recipients of such incentives and require the Municipal Mayor to require the Budget Officer to provide appropriations in the Personal Services account for all payment of honoraria/incentive and other forms of personnel benefits, and the Municipal Accountant to take up all disbursements under the Personal Services account.
observations.managementAction: AAR Fully Implemented 2016 Refund of amount received by the recipients through payroll deductions have been initiated/completed.

Henceforth, require the RCCs to remit daily and intact their collections and report them on the period they were collected.

Further, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer submit an explanation why the Cash in Vault-General Fund (101) has an abnormal or negative balance and prepare the necessary adjusting entry in compliance with COA Cir. No. 2004-008 dated September 20, 2004 and Section 111 of PD 1445. Henceforth, a periodic reconciliation should be made.
observations.managementAction: AAR Fully Implemented 2014 All collections were deposited intact by the Municipal Treasurer. Necessary adjustments have already been made to reflect the correct amount of cash local treasury account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Treasurer to intensify the collection to recover government funds invested in
the program and instruct the Municipal Accountant to institute action for the
collection of the accounts or other appropriate remedies to clear the amount
in the books

We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to determine the reasons on why each fund transfer account balance were not
liquidated and undertake appropriate action. If warranted, request/apply for
the write-off of the accounts following the guidelines under COA Circular No.
2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to institute action for the collection of
the accounts or other appropriate remedies to clear the amount in the
books;
b. Analyze each fund transfer account balance and determine the reason
why these were not liquidated and undertake immediate appropriate
action thereon; and
c. If warranted, request/apply for the write-off of the accounts following the
guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Give priority for the creation of an Inventory Committee to conduct the
physical count of PPE.
b) Direct the head of the Municipal Treasurer Office and the Municipal
Accounting Office to hasten the updating of their records to facilitate the
reconciliation of the accounting and property records.
c) Oblige the Municipal Accountant to review the details of properties
which are non-functional or unserviceable and prepare the necessary
adjustments, if warranted.
d) Require the committee to prepare a methodical and well-planned
physical inventory taking, which could be done in a year round basis,
until all the Property, Plant and Equipment of the municipality have
been accounted for and listed in the Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). Also, all AREs must be
renewed regularly pursuant to existing rules and regulations.
e) Thereafter, the RPCPPE must be reconciled with the property record of
each class of property at the General Services Office and subsidiary
ledgers maintained at the Accounting Office. Any discrepancy should be
investigated and appropriate action should be taken thereof.
f) Make representation with the previous accounting personnel to
substantiate and reconcile the forwarded balances prior to CY 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Petty Cash custodian
to effect the immediate liquidation of the petty cash fund.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Bank
Reconciliation Statements with the bank statements and original
copies of paid checks as prescribed under Section 74 of PD 1445 and
COA Circular No. 96-011;
b) Analyze the discrepancy noted between the book and bank confirmed
balances; and
c) Make necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the cash
accounts.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a) Treasury Office to update the RPTARs/ Taxpayers Index Card and
subsequently, prepare and furnish the Accounting Office the list required
under Section 20 Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and
b) Municipal Accountant to establish RPT and SET receivables sourcing from
the list duly certified by the Treasury Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a) Property Custodian to update, prepare and submit all documents such
as the duly approved RIS to evidence the issuance of the materials from
stock and Report of Supplies and Materials Issued as basis for crediting
the non-existing balance from the books;
b) Municipal Accountant to refrain from recording consumed inventory in the
books and reconcile accounting and property and supply records; and
c) Adjust the non-existing and consumed unaccounted materials inventory
in the books.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant:

Comply with the provisions of Sections 43 of the Manual of the NGAS,
Volume I on the proper recording of purchases and issuances of supplies
and materials; and
Maintain the Subsidiary Ledger Card (SLC) for inventories.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
record all purchases of inventory items under the perpetual inventory
method in its related inventory account and also the Municipal Social Welfare
Officer and Treasurer to furnish the copy of delivery receipt/acceptance and
inspection report of all received inventory items.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to recognize the expired portion from the prepayments and prepare
periodically liquidation report of all prepayments.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the Intra-Agency Accounts, Due from Other Funds and
Due to Other Funds to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances of the
two accounts and effect the necessary adjustments, if appropriate, to bring the
affected accounts into an agreement.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the Local School Board
to come up with a strategic prioritization plan in the allocation of the SEF to
public schools taking into consideration the most important needs of the
students and not just for repairs of school facilities for optimum attainment of
the intent of the fund in accordance with DepEd-DDBM-DLG JC 2017-001.

We recommended that LDRRMO under the direction of the Local Chief
Executive to prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization
of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the department heads
concerned to comply with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2014-1 that the three (3) DRRM positions be filled immediately.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct Municipal Accountant to
strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines for LDRRM 
properly record all transactions effected under this fund.

We recommended that Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant
ensure that only expenditures enumerated under the DILG-DBM JMC No.
2017-01 are considered in the charging against the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the HRMO to submit a
record of the JO employees detailing the expected work to be performed and/or
output and require each payee to prepare and submit the accomplishment
report duly signed by the immediate supervisor. We further recommend that
the Local Chief Executive instruct the HRMO to prepare the manning
schedule, specifically, the laborers/cleaners and ensure that the daily time
record reflect the actual hours worked by an employee.

We recommended that LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer to remit the taxes withheld intact to lessen the liability and
accountability of the LGU, and prevent the possibility of using the funds for other
purposes.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the schedules or list of some General Ledgers accounts and the corresponding Subsidiary Ledgers as well as the supporting documents for the entries in the General Journals and the computations for the depreciation expenses. Likewise, the General Ledger accounts should have monthly balances which will reconcile with the amounts in the monthly trial balances. Henceforth, strictly follow the accounting rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation:
Since fiscal responsibility rests upon the  Head  of  the  Agency,  we
 recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer  to
 render her accounts and submit the required financial transactions together
 with supporting documents to the Accounting  Division,  and  the  Municipal
 Accountant to submit the accounts and reports to COA on or before the  10th
 day of the succeeding month.


      Likewise, the accountable officer and/or other officials concerned are
 reminded of the sanctions imposed under Sec. 2 of PD 1455  in  relation  to
 Art. 218 of the Revised Penal Code on the automatic suspension  of  payment
 of salaries for failure to render accounts in a period of two months.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should admonish the  Local
Accountant  and  Budget  Officer  to  be  responsive  to  their  duties  and
responsibilities.  The  maintenance  and  recording  of  appropriation   and
allotment received, obligation incurred and disbursed is a must  to  prevent
incurrence  of  obligations  and  disbursements  in  excess   of   allotment
received.

We recommend that Municipal Accountant  analyze  and  investigate  the
causes of the negative and abnormal balances and  record  the  corresponding
adjustments to correct the errors.


      We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Petty Cash  custodian
to effect the immediate liquidation of the petty cash fund.


      We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to:

     a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to  prepare  and  submit  the  bank
        reconciliation statements with  the  bank  statement  and  original
        copies of paid checks as prescribed under Section 74 of PD 1445 and
        COA Circular No. 96-011.


     b) Analyze the discrepancy noted between the book and  bank  confirmed
        balances.


     c) Make necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances  of  the
        cash accounts.

We recommend that Municipal Mayor:


     a.  Instruct the Municipal  Accountant  to  institute  action  for  the
        collection of the accounts or other appropriate  remedies  to  clear
        the amount in the books;


     b. Analyze each fund transfer account balance and determine the  reason
        why these were not liquidated and  undertake  immediate  appropriate
        action thereon; and


     c. If warranted,  request/apply  for  the  write-off  of  the  accounts
        following the guidelines under  COA  Circular  No.  2016-005,  dated
        December 19, 2016.

We recommend to compel the  General  Services  Officer  and  Municipal
Accountant to:

     1. Prepare and submit all documents and reports such as duly  approved
        RIS to evidence the issuance of the materials from stock and Report
        of Supplies and Materials Issued etc. as basis  for  crediting  the
        non--existing balance from the books;


     2. Direct the General Services Officer or the Municipal  Treasurer  to
        prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI)  weekly
        as a basis by the Accounting Unit in preparing the  JEV  to  record
        the supplies and materials issued;


     3.  Adjust  the  non-existing  and  consumed   unaccounted   materials
        inventory in the books.


      We recommend that the Municipal Accountant:

     1. Comply with the provisions of Sections 43  of  the  Manual  of  the
        NGAS, Volume I on the proper recording of purchases  and  issuances
        of supplies and materials;


     2.  Restore  to  inventory  accounts  the  undocumented  P2,156,888.60
        adjustments; and


     3. Maintain the Subsidiary Ledger Card (SLC) for inventories;


      We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:

   a) Require the General Services Office  or  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
      conduct inventory taking periodically and submit the inventory  report
      for periodic reconciliation of records between  the  General  Services
      and Accounting Unit;


   b) Require the General Services Officer or the  Municipal  Treasurer  and
      Municipal Accountant to maintain and update regularly the SCs and SLCs
      for  each  inventory  item,  regularly  reconcile  their  records  and
      immediately effect the necessary adjustments in the books of  accounts
      and/or supply records for any differences noted;


   c) Submit the RSMI and RIS as supporting document  to  the  issuances  of
      inventory as drawn in the general journal;


   d) Require the Accounting Office and the signing official in the purchase
      order, to supply needed information in the disbursement  vouchers  and
      purchase orders; and


   e) Classify and record transactions to appropriate accounts;

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:


     a) Give priority for the creation of an Inventory Committee to conduct
        the physical count of PPE.


     b) Direct Head of  the  General  Services  Office  and  the  Municipal
        Accounting Office to hasten the updating of records  to  facilitate
        the reconciliation of the accounting and property records.


     c) Oblige the Municipal Accountant to review the details of properties
        which are non-functional or unserviceable and prepare the necessary
        adjustments, if warranted.


     d) Require the committee to prepare  a  methodical  and  well  planned
        physical inventory taking, which could be  done  in  a  year  round
        basis,  until  all  the  Property,  Plant  and  Equipment  of   the
        municipality are accounted for and listed  in  the  Report  on  the
        Physical Count of Property, Plant and  Equipment  (RPCPPE).   Also,
        all AREs must be renewed regularly pursuant to existing  rules  and
        regulations.


     e) Thereafter, the RPCPPE must be reconciled with the property  record
        of each class of  property  at  the  General  Services  Office  and
        subsidiary  ledgers  maintained  at  the  Accounting  Office.   Any
        discrepancy should be investigated and appropriate action should be
        taken thereof.


     f) Make representation  with  the  previous  accounting  personnel  to
        substantiate and reconcile the forwarded balances prior to CY 2012.


      We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the  concerned  Municipal
officials to work out and to see to it that  Titles  of  the  purchased  and
donated parcels of land  are  transferred  in  the  name  of  the  Municipal
Government of Lavezares to establish absolute ownership thereof.


      We recommend strict  compliance  with  the  accounting  and  reporting
guidelines for  local  road  network  construction  and  maintenance  system
prescribed by COA Circular No. 2015-008.


      We recommended that the Accountant draw a JEV to transfer the costs of
completed projects from CIP-Infrastructure Assets  and  Building  and  Other
Structures account to Infrastructure Assets and Building/School building  or
Other structures account.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Budget  Officer  and  Municipal
Accountant ensure that   only expenditures  enumerated  under  the  DILG-DBM
JMC No. 2011-1 are considered in the charging against  the  20%  Development
Fund.

We recommend that the LSB develop  a  comprehensive  budget  based  on
logical framework of program to ensure that all  components  and  activities
are performed in line with the programmed design and that is  achieving  its
objectives.

      We recommend the Municipal Accountant  to  strictly  observe   the
provision of Section 6 of DEPED/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No.  01,  series  of
2017 in the preparation and submission of  the  quarterly  and  annual  fund
utilization reports.

We recommend strict compliance with  COA  Circular  No.2012-002  dated
September 12, 2012 to ensure that a minimum  of  5%  be  allocated  for  the
LDRRMF.

      We recommend strict observance to the provision of Section  5.1.5  COA
Circular No. 2012-002 requiring submission of the report on  or  before  the
15th day after the end of each month to the COA auditor of the LGU.

      We recommend that the positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC
Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April  4,  2014  be  filled  up
immediately  by  the   Municipality   to   properly   institutionalize   the
organization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office.

      We recommend  that  LGU  strictly  implement  the  provisions  of  COA
Circular No. 2012-002 with regard to appropriation and  utilization  of  the
5% of the revenue from the regular sources  for  disaster  preparedness  and
mitigation.

      We recommend that management  efficiently  utilize  the  allotted  70%
Mitigation Fund. Likewise direct the  Municipal  Accountant  to  follow  and
submit  the  same  in  the  prescribed  format,  recording   and   reporting
guidelines.

      We recommend the management to  observe  strict  compliance  with  the
LDRRMF guidelines set forth under Section 5 of COA  Circular  No.  2012-002,
dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that management formulate an action plan to implement the
audit recommendations, and submit immediately the  duly  accomplished  AAPSI
form to the Audit Team, copy furnished the DBM, the House  of  Committee  on
Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance pursuant  to  Section  93  of
R.A. 10651.

We recommend that the LGU strictly adhere to the specific provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 and Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 on the accounting, reporting and utilization of the LDRRMF. Furthermore, we specifically require the submission of distribution lists for procured relief goods and medicines, reports on the receipts and distribution/utilization/issuance and other necessary supporting documents of in-kind donations received from DSWD RO. VIII.

Require the Municipal
Accountant to submit the documents herein mentioned in order to include such transactions in the determination of the total expenditures for the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the schedules or list of some General Ledgers accounts and the corresponding Subsidiary Ledgers as well as the supporting documents for the entries in the General Journals and the computations for the depreciation expenses. Likewise, the General Ledger accounts should have monthly balances which will reconcile with the amounts in the monthly trial balances. Henceforth, strictly follow the accounting rules and regulations.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare Reconciliation between actual amounts on a comparable basis as presented in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and in the Statement of Financial Performance. Likewise, include data for the Special Education Fund in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts. Henceforth, strictly comply the provisions of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in the preparation of the financial statements.

We recommend that management submit the lacking supporting documents; rectify the deficiencies noted; require the contractors/suppliers to issue official receipt in gross amount; and implement projects within the specified contract time. Henceforth, refrain from procuring thru reimbursements, payment without complete documentations, and strictly comply the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR.

We also recommend that management submit justification for the early period of action on procurement activities and why COA was not informed of the deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance.

Likewise, we recommend that official responsible for accepting deliveries of procured items furnish COA with delivery documents within twenty-four (24) hours after such acceptance.

These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension, Notice of Disallowance and Notice of Charge as a result of evaluation of the documents and justifications to be submitted by the municipality.

We recommend that management submit the required Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreement of the different projects as well as the Project Briefs and Local Poverty Reduction Action Plan (LPRAP) for the livelihood projects. Likewise, submit the lacking supporting documents and justifications as well as rectify the other deficiencies noted. Further, we recommend that projects be fully implemented upon receipt of the funds for the benefit of the constituents/beneficiaries.

These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Notice of Disallowance as a result of post audit of the documents and justifications to be submitted by the municipality.

We recommend that management efficiently utilize the 70% Mitigation Fund of the LDRRMF, rectify the deficiencies noted and submit the lacking supporting documents. Likewise, we recommend that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer prepare the LDRRMFIP and the other required reports and submit the same in the prescribed format and period; the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the prescribed recording and reporting guidelines; and the Property Officer maintain separate registry and prepare the required monthly reports on the receipt and issuance of in-kind donations.

These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Notice of Disallowance as a result of the post audit of the required documents.

We recommend that management refrain from granting financial assistance, utilize the fund to those expenses specified by law, strictly monitor the expenditures not to exceed the budgeted amount, submit the lacking supporting documents, rectify the other deficiencies noted and implement all items in the budget. Henceforth, strictly adhere the provisions of Sections 100 (c) and 272 of RA 7160 and Section 1 of RA 5447 with regards to the utilization of the Special Education Fund.

We recommend that the concerned officials and employees submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers, payrolls and liquidation reports with supporting documents, the cancelled check, a report on the actual status of checks noted as gaps in the series of checks issued and the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms.  Henceforth, management should adhere strictly with the pertinent provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Disallowance as a result of non-compliance and/or audit of the documents to be submitted.
We recommend that management require the recipient officials and employees to refund the amount. Henceforth, adhere strictly with the provisions of AO No. 263 on the granting of anniversary bonus.

We recommend that management furnish the audit team with a complete set of the annual budget with the Annual Investment Program and the advice on the review by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan and Local Finance Committee of the Province of Northern Samar on the CY 2016 Annual Budget and the appropriation ordinances. Likewise, reconcile the amount stated in the annual budget and the SAAOB. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of Sec. 327 of RA 7160 or the Local Government Code.

We recommend that the LGU strictly adhere to the specific provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 and Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 on the accounting, reporting and utilization of the LDRRMF. Furthermore, we specifically require the submission of distribution lists for procured relief goods and medicines, reports on the receipts and distribution/utilization/issuance and other necessary supporting documents of in-kind donations received from DSWD RO. observations.reason: The LGU still hired consultants.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU assess its existing organizational structure so that the ideal number of plantilla personnel will be established, thereby, avoiding the hiring of excessive number of job order personnel. We further recommend that the hiring of job order personnel be resorted to based on necessity in order to avoid waste of much needed funds, as prescribed in Section 76 of R.A. 7160. Likewise, we recommend that the LGU strictly adhere to the policies and guidelines in the hiring of personnel under contract of services and job orders as provided for in CSC Memorandum No. 17, s. 2002, promulgating CSC Resolution No. 02-0790 dated June 5, 2002.
observations.reason: A number of job order personnel were still hired.
